title: Housekeepers and related occupations
description: Provide personalized assistance to individuals with disabilities or illness who require help with personal care and activities of daily living support (e.g., feeding, bathing, dressing, grooming, toileting, and ambulation). May also provide help with tasks such as preparing meals, doing light housekeeping, and doing laundry. Work is performed in various settings depending on the needs of the care recipient and may include locations such as their home, place of work, out in the community, or at a daytime nonresidential facility. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food. Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Ensure food is stored and cooked at the correct temperature by regulating the temperature of ovens, broilers, grills, and roasters.
Wash pots, pans, dishes, utensils, or other cooking equipment.
Plan and prepare food for parties, holiday meals, luncheons, special functions, and other social events while adhering to menu plans.
Train new employees and supervise the work of kitchen staff to ensure quality standards and service.
Cook foodstuffs according to menus, considering dietary restrictions and nutritional needs, and serve meals to clients or families.
Perform housekeeping duties including cooking, cleaning, washing clothes or dishes, and running errands.
Inspect and clean food preparation areas, ensuring safety and sanitary food-handling practices are followed.
Monitor and record food temperatures to ensure food safety, and inspect supplies and equipment for quality.
Prepare and maintain records of client progress, services performed, and necessary reporting of client condition to supervisors.
Provide clients with personal care and assistance, including ambulation, personal hygiene, and companionship during recovery.
Recruit and hire staff members to ensure appropriate staffing levels.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies.
Assign work schedules, following work requirements, to ensure quality and timely delivery of service.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Take disciplinary action to address performance problems when necessary.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
Requisition necessary supplies, equipment, or services to facilitate operations.
Inform workers about the interests or special needs of specific groups to enhance service delivery.
Direct or coordinate the activities of workers, such as hotel staff or recreation staff, to ensure efficient operations.
Analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports.
